narrative:
Sunrise temperatures on mountaintops are 10 degrees warmer than in deeper valleys. The mountaintops point the way to our near-term future. This afternoon is 5–6 degrees warmer than Tuesday afternoon. Add another 10 degrees Thursday afternoon. Seasonally warm temperatures last into Friday, but a front moves through Friday night. Scattered showers and thundershowers arrive later Friday afternoon and continue into Friday evening. Colder air arrives Friday night, and Saturday is much cooler but dry.

Trending Warmer through Friday

Last Updated Wed March 25 at 10:52AM by Ray Russell

Sunrise temperatures on mountaintops are 10 degrees warmer than in deeper valleys. The mountaintops point the way to our near-term future. This afternoon is 5–6 degrees warmer than Tuesday afternoon. Add another 10 degrees Thursday afternoon. Seasonally warm temperatures last into Friday, but a front moves through Friday night. Scattered showers and thundershowers arrive later Friday afternoon and continue into Friday evening. Colder air arrives Friday night, and Saturday is much cooler but dry.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

Overall, we are in a dry, warm pattern, but we have a brief, cool “hiccup” this weekend.

High pressure shifts east into the Western Atlantic, allowing a cool wedge along the east side of the Appalachians to erode today. After a cool start in valleys, temperatures moderate this afternoon. Thursday is considerably warmer, with a “barely worth mentioning” chance of a renegade afternoon shower.

A cold front arrives from the north Friday evening. Scattered showers and thundershowers develop later Friday afternoon and continue into Friday evening. The western half of the Appalachians has somewhat more rain than the eastern half.

Colder air arrives later Friday night. Precipitation could end as flurries at higher elevations after midnight.

Saturday has clearing skies and cool temperatures. High pressure centers in Illinois Saturday, then moves east this weekend, briefly forming a cool wedge down the east side of the Appalachians. Expect morning frost Sunday, followed by slightly milder temperatures in the afternoon.

We warm up again Monday and Tuesday in a more southerly flow. A shower is not impossible Monday and Tuesday afternoon, but there is too much uncertainty to include that in the forecast at this time. A more prolonged period of warm weather follows.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.
